headline: Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
subhead: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.

headline: 'I almost faint every time I stand up': In cramped student housing, studying becomes impossible during the heatwave
How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

headline: Iran intensifies internal crackdown as US and Israel wage war on regime
subhead: While diplomatic talks between Tehran and Washington focus on Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, NGOs are warning that executions and mass arrests continue.

headline: Sam Barlow: The art of fragmented storytelling from the creator of 'Her Story' and 'Immortality'
subhead: Since 'Her Story' (2015), the video game designer has subverted traditional narrative mechanics, immersing players in a profusion of video fragments and leaving them to piece the story back together.

headline: Keith Haring's final work finds a home in Paris's 'artists' church'
subhead: The parish at Saint-Eustache in Paris is unveiling the restored Saint-Vincent-de-Paul chapel, funded by the World Monuments Fund. The renewed setting gives fresh prominence to 'The Life of Christ,' the American artist's final work before his death in 1990, during the AIDS epidemic.

France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

headline: Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
subhead: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.

headline: Masculinism, an increasingly widespread, violent and popular ideology among French youth
subhead: According to a report written by the Sénat's delegation for women's rights and published on Wednesday, the movement, which has close ties to the far right, is now considered an emerging terrorist threat and a cause for serious concern.
